handling_steps: Flatten the cardboard box to save space.
Keep it dry — wet cardboard cannot be recycled.
Remove any plastic tape or labels.
Place in the paper/cardboard recycling bin.
Rinse the glass container to remove residue.
Do not mix with mirrors, window glass, or ceramics.
Place in the glass recycling bin if available.
Wrap broken glass in newspaper before disposal.
Rinse cans and containers.
Crush cans to reduce volume.
Aluminum and steel are both recyclable.
Place in the metal recycling bin.
Keep paper clean and dry.
Shred sensitive documents if needed.
Avoid recycling wax-coated or greasy paper.
Place in the paper recycling bin.
Check the resin code (1, 2, 5 are widely recyclable).
Rinse containers to remove food residue.
Replace caps back on bottles.
Place in the plastic recycling bin.
If contaminated with food, dispose in general waste.
Consider if any part is recyclable.
Use a sealed bag to prevent odors.
Place in the general waste bin.
NEVER throw in regular trash.
Store in a cool, dry place away from metal objects.
Tape the terminals of lithium batteries.
Drop off at designated battery collection points.
